name: reverse-SynthID
readme_summary: This repository reverse-engineers Google's SynthID AI watermarking system using signal processing and spectral analysis. It discovers the watermark's structure, builds a detector with 90% accuracy, and develops a multi-resolution spectral bypass to surgically remove the watermark.
key_features: Spectral analysis for detection and removal
Achieves 75% carrier energy drop and 91% phase coherence drop
primary_use_case: Detecting and removing invisible AI watermarks embedded in images by Google's SynthID system.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled digital alchemist, wearing futuristic goggles, stands before a complex spectral analysis console. They are holding a glowing, ethereal image in one hand, revealing faint, intricate patterns within its light. With the other hand, they manipulate holographic sliders and waveforms on the console, causing the patterns within the image to visibly dissolve and disappear, leaving a pristine, clear image. Small, abstract representations of carrier frequencies and phase coherence metrics float around the scene as subtle data visualizations. The overall mood is one of precise scientific discovery and digital restoration, rendered in a clean, high-tech 3D isometric illustration style with a cool blue and purple color palette.

name: developer-icons
description: A collection of well-optimized SVG tech logos for developers and designers—customizable, scalable, and free.
readme_summary: This repository provides a collection of well-optimized SVG logos for various technologies, designed for developers and designers. The icons are customizable, scalable, and available as an npm package, making them easy to integrate into projects.
key_features: Collection of well-optimized SVG tech logos
Compatible with TypeScript, React, and Next.js
primary_use_case: To provide developers and designers with a readily available and easily integrable set of technology logos for use in websites, applications, and design projects.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant digital workshop where a skilled artisan is meticulously crafting and assembling a diverse array of polished, glowing technology logos. The artisan uses specialized tools that emit streams of clean vector data, transforming raw digital energy into recognizable icons like 'React', 'Astro', and 'Tailwind CSS'. In the background, a large, clean interface displays a catalog of these icons, ready to be picked and used. Subtle code snippets and data visualizations float around the scene, indicating the technical nature of the icons. The overall mood is creative and productive, with a bright, clean color palette and a 3D isometric illustration style.

name: livestore
description: LiveStore is a next-generation state management framework based on reactive SQLite and built-in sync engine.
readme_summary: LiveStore is a next-generation state management framework that provides a powerful, client-centric data foundation for applications. It leverages reactive SQLite and a built-in sync engine to enable true offline-first workflows with custom merge conflict resolution and adapters for various platforms.
key_features: Reactive query layer with full SQLite support
Adapters for web, mobile, server/edge, desktop
Flexible data modeling and schema management
Sync with supported providers or custom solutions
primary_use_case: To serve as a client-centric data layer for applications, enabling offline-first capabilities and real-time data synchronization.
cover_image_prompt: A bustling, vibrant marketplace where merchants (representing different application clients) are seamlessly exchanging goods (data) with each other, even when the marketplace itself (the network) is temporarily closed or experiencing disruptions. Each merchant has a personal, well-organized stall (local SQLite database) that remains fully functional. A central, magical ledger (LiveStore sync engine) instantly updates all transactions across the marketplace once connections are re-established, ensuring no data is lost and conflicts are resolved harmoniously. Small, glowing icons representing 'sync' and 'offline' float subtly around the scene. The overall style is a warm, inviting 3D isometric illustration with clear, friendly characters and a focus on smooth, interconnected activity.

name: Baileys
description: Socket-based TS/JavaScript API for WhatsApp Web
readme_summary: Baileys is a TypeScript library for interacting with the WhatsApp Web API using WebSockets. It allows developers to build applications that can send and receive messages, manage contacts, and interact with WhatsApp features programmatically.
primary_use_case: Developing custom applications and bots that integrate with WhatsApp.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan, representing the Baileys library, meticulously weaving a complex tapestry of digital threads (representing WhatsApp messages and data). The artisan is seated at a workbench, with glowing threads flowing from a WhatsApp icon on one side and being intricately woven into a vibrant, organized pattern on the other. Subtle UI elements showing message bubbles and contact icons are integrated into the tapestry. The scene is set in a clean, modern workshop with soft, focused lighting, emphasizing the creation of order from raw data. The overall style is a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, digital aesthetic, highlighting the library's role in structuring and managing WhatsApp communication.

name: nowinandroid
description: A fully functional Android app built entirely with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose
readme_summary: Now in Android is a fully functional Android application built with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose, demonstrating best practices in Android development. It serves as a reference for developers and provides up-to-date news and content about the Android ecosystem.
key_features: Built entirely with Kotlin and Jetpack Compose
Follows Android design and development best practices
Provides regular news updates about Android development
Allows users to browse content like videos and articles
Enables users to follow topics and receive notifications
primary_use_case: To serve as a reference for Android development best practices using Kotlin and Jetpack Compose, and to keep developers informed about the latest Android news and content.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, modern Android robot character holding a magnifying glass over a glowing, constantly updating stream of digital news feeds and articles. The robot is surrounded by floating icons representing Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, and Android development. In the background, subtle UI elements of the app are visible, like a stylized 'For You' feed and topic icons. The overall scene should convey a sense of discovery and staying informed within the Android development world. The art style should be a clean, 3D isometric illustration with bright, engaging colors and a focus on clarity and modern design, with the 'Now in Android' logo subtly integrated.
